message:
feat(claudeconfig): snapshot/restore Claude Code model setting (#110)

Introduces internal/claudeconfig/, a small stdlib-only package providing
atomic read/write of the Claude Code CLI's persistent settings.json
`model` field plus Snapshot()/Restore() helpers. This is the foundation
for ensuring that after a swarm finishes, the user's pre-launch model
preference is put back — so opening a post-swarm interactive Claude
Code session no longer surfaces the swarm's last chosen model.

Package design notes:

- Atomic writes. Both WriteModel and Snapshot stage to a tmpfile in
  the destination directory, fsync, then rename. A crash mid-write
  cannot leave a truncated settings.json or snapshot behind.

- Preserves sibling fields. WriteModel round-trips any other keys
  in settings.json (theme, font_size, MCP server config, etc.)
  rather than clobbering them — implemented by unmarshal into
  map[string]any, mutate the single key, marshal back.

- No-op on the empty path. WriteModel(path, "") with settings.json
  absent does NOT create an empty file: if the user never had a
  settings.json to begin with, "restoring" their pre-swarm state
  means not materializing one. A snapshotted "no model field set"
  is expressed via the HadModelField=false flag so Restore can
  distinguish "user had no model" (remove the swarm's value) from
  "snapshot never ran" (absent snapshot file → Restore is a no-op).

- Honors CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR. ResolveClaudeSettingsPath() matches the
  same env-var contract the upstream Claude Code CLI exposes, so a
  user with side-by-side accounts (and therefore several .claude
  dirs) gets the right settings.json. Same pattern as
  coding_agent_usage_tracker#6.

- Idempotent Restore. Restore(snapshotPath) on an absent snapshot
  is a no-op, so it is safe to wire into every shutdown code path
  (graceful shutdown, force-kill, signal handlers) without
  inter-path coordination.

14 unit tests cover: env-var resolution, absent / empty / malformed
/ missing-field / present `model` field parsing, WriteModel
preserving other fields, empty-string removing the field, the
"don't create brand-new empty settings.json" invariant, the
roundtrip with `model` initially set, the roundtrip with `model`
initially absent (must strip the swarm's value), and idempotent
Restore on a missing snapshot.

Wiring into swarm spawn / GracefulShutdown is deliberately a
follow-on commit so the library can land and bake — see the
package doc comment for the intended integration points.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
